summary: High‑energy set with killer Bertha suite and soaring Terrapin>Watchtower
blurb: The July 4, 1989 show bursts out of the gate with a blistering Bertha → Greatest Story → Cold Rain & Snow opening. Highlights include a fiery Terrapin Station into Watchtower stretch, strong vocal interplay on Not Fade Away, and an uplifting U.S. Blues encore.
review: Rich Stadium on Independence Day 1989 delivered a punchy, high‑octane performance that still feels fresh for Deadheads. The first set kicks off with the classic Bertha → Greatest Story Ever Told → Cold Rain And Snow suite, setting a bright tone that carries through tight renditions of Walkin' Blues, Row Jimmy and a soulful When I Paint My Masterpiece. Jerry’s lead work shines on Terrapin Station and he trades vocal lines with Brent on Not Fade Away, while Brent’s keyboards stay solid and above‑average throughout the night. The second set builds momentum with Touch of Grey into Man Smart, Woman Smarter, then launches into an expansive Playing In The Band → Terrapin Station → Drums → Space → I Will Take You Home → All Along The Watchtower → Morning Dew → Not Fade Away sequence that many reviewers called "awesome" and "high‑energy." The show closes on a joyous U.S. Blues encore, leaving the crowd buzzing. Across multiple audience, soundboard, and matrix sources the performance is consistently praised, with only minor technical hiccups (a few skips on Man Smart) and occasional comments about Jerry’s voice sounding a bit froggier than usual. Overall, it’s an above‑average late‑80s outing that showcases Brent’s vocal chemistry with Jerry and delivers several must‑hear moments for any Deadhead collection.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (5 out of 7) are marked positive, repeatedly highlighting standout songs and high‑energy playing. Multiple sources praise the opening Bertha suite, Terrapin Station/Watchtower stretch, and Brent’s vocal interplay, while only minor mixed notes (skips on Man Smart, a weary vocal tone) appear. Consistent enthusiasm yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good audience tape with solid sound after light EQ, captures the full high‑energy performance and includes most of the highlighted songs.

key_highlights: Opening Bertha → Greatest Story Ever Told → Cold Rain & Snow suite launches the night with power.
Terrapin Station into a massive Watchtower/Space jam in the second set showcases tight group interplay.
Brent’s vocal and keyboard work shines on Not Fade Away and throughout the set, especially alongside Jerry.
U.S. Blues encore delivers an uplifting close that leaves the crowd buzzing.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers strong lead guitar throughout, with standout work on Terrapin Station and a solid slide on Walkin' Blues; his voice is noted as a bit weary but still carries the set.
band_performance.Bob: Provides powerful vocals on When I Paint My Masterpiece, Looks Like Rain and Deal, stretching to meet the song’s demands.
band_performance.Brent: Earns frequent praise for vocal interplay with Jerry (especially on Not Fade Away) and solid, above‑average keyboard work that drives the jams.
